per_question.prediction: A first plagiarism offence results in a written warning and mandatory completion of the Academic Integrity Workshop within 30 days.
A second plagiarism offence within the same academic year results in failure of the course and a notation on the student's academic record that remains visible for two years.
carries an automatic course failure and academic probation for one semester.
including fabrication of experimental results or selective reporting of outcomes
Appeals against academic integrity sanctions must be submitted in writing to the Office of the Registrar within 14 calendar days of the decision being communicated.
Faculty members are required to report suspected violations of academic integrity within five business days of discovery using the official incident form available on the registrar portal.
Withdrawal after this deadline results in a grade of F.
are assigned only when a student has completed at least 70 percent of coursework but cannot finish due to documented medical or family emergency.
Incomplete coursework must be submitted within six weeks of the start of the following semester.
but all attempts remain on the transcript.
A minimum cumulative GPA of 2.0 is required to remain in good academic standing.
Students placed on academic probation must meet with their academic advisor within the first two weeks of the probationary semester and may not enroll in more than 12 credit hours.
00 PM on weekends during the regular semester.
Undergraduate students may borrow up to ten books at a time for a loan period of 21 days.
Undergraduate students may borrow up to ten books at a time for a loan period of 21 days.
provided no other patron has placed a hold on the item.
capped at 50 SAR per item.
Patrons with unpaid fines exceeding 100 SAR have their borrowing privileges suspended until cleared.
Each reservation has a maximum duration of three hours per day per student.
Withdrawal during the second week qualifies for a 75 percent refund of tuition.
Withdrawal during the second week qualifies for a 75 percent refund of tuition.
All refunds are credited to the original payment method within fifteen business days of the withdrawal being processed by the registrar.
and laboratory fees are non-refundable after the first week of classes.
IRB applications must be submitted at least six weeks before the planned start date of data collection.
Research data collected from human participants must be stored on encrypted university servers for a minimum of five years after publication.
000 SAR in annual value must be disclosed in the Annual Conflict of Interest report submitted by January 31 each year.
The Merit Excellence Scholarship covers 100 percent of tuition for undergraduate students with a cumulative GPA of 3.75 or higher and is renewable annually.
000 SAR per semester to students who demonstrate financial need through the standardized application.
500 SAR plus tuition waiver in exchange for twenty hours of research work per week.
000 SAR per student per academic year.
Master's thesis defenses must be scheduled at least three weeks in advance and require a committee of three faculty members including the supervisor and one external examiner.
Doctoral dissertation defenses must be scheduled at least six weeks in advance and require a committee of five faculty members
The defense begins with a public presentation of approximately 30 minutes for master's students and 45 minutes for doctoral candidates
pass with major revisions requiring resubmission within 90 days
and currently enrolled students are eligible for VPN access.
install the GlobalProtect client from the IT downloads page and use 'vpn.university.edu' as the portal address.
VPN sessions automatically time out after eight hours of activity or two hours of inactivity.
meaning only traffic destined for university subnets is routed through the VPN.
When a mailbox reaches 90 percent of its quota a warning email is sent.
At 100 percent the mailbox stops accepting new incoming messages.
after which they are permanently removed.
The 'UnivWiFi-Guest' network is open but rate-limited to 5 Mbps per device and requires daily reauthentication through the captive portal.
Passwords must be at least 12 characters long and contain a mix of uppercase letters
Passwords expire every 180 days.
Account lockout occurs after five consecutive failed authentication attempts within 15 minutes.
Lockout is automatically lifted after 30 minutes or can be cleared by IT support after identity verification.
an initial situation report must be communicated to the CIO within two hours.
Suspected security incidents must be reported to security@university.edu or the on-call number within one hour of discovery.
90 days for daily backups
Restore time objective is four hours for critical systems and 24 hours for standard systems.
Personal device backups are not provided by IT.
Each undergraduate student receives a printing quota of 500 pages per semester.
Color printing counts as three pages against the quota for each color page printed.
Print jobs are held in the print queue for two hours before being deleted unreleased.
Adobe Creative Cloud
which meets twice per month.
License audits are conducted annually.
Approval typically takes two business days
Reporting persistent connection issues requires the MAC address of the affected device.
Both methods require a one-time code valid for 10 minutes.
The incident response team operates on a four-tier severity scale
Test restores are performed quarterly for at least one critical system to validate backup integrity.
The price is 5 SAR per 100 pages for monochrome printing.
Full-time staff are entitled to 30 working days of annual leave per fiscal year.
Annual leave is accrued at a rate of 2.5 days per month of completed service for staff.
Annual leave is accrued at a rate of 2.5 days per month of completed service for staff.
Up to ten unused leave days may be carried over into the following fiscal year.
accrued but unused leave is paid out at the employee's base salary rate
Full-time employees are entitled to 30 days of paid sick leave per calendar year.
Sick leave of more than three consecutive days requires a medical certificate from an accredited physician.
An additional 60 days of half-pay sick leave may be granted for serious or chronic conditions
Employees who require more than 90 days of consecutive sick leave may apply for an extended medical leave of absence of up to one year.
All employees undergo a formal performance review once per fiscal year
Employees rated 1 or 2 are placed on a Performance Improvement Plan with measurable goals and a review date 90 days later.
which is calibrated by the compensation committee in June and applied to July paychecks.
Female employees are entitled to ten weeks of fully paid maternity leave around the birth of a child.
Leave may begin up to four weeks before the expected due date.
to be taken within thirty days of the birth.
Adoption of a child under the age of three carries the same paid leave entitlements as biological parenthood
All open positions are posted on the university careers portal for at least fifteen business days before applications close.
References are conducted by phone and documented in writing.
Offer letters are valid for ten business days from the date of signing by the Provost or Chief Operating Officer.
Cash gifts of any amount are prohibited.
Acceptance of gifts from vendors with a value exceeding 500 SAR must be reported on the Annual Disclosure Form.
Base salaries are paid monthly on the 27th of each month.
Overtime is paid at 1.5 times the hourly rate for hours worked beyond 48 in a week.
are typically paid in December and are calibrated based on individual performance ratings and overall university financial performance.
Salary advances of up to two months of base pay may be requested in writing for documented hardship.

Full-time staff are entitled to 30 working days of annual leave per fiscal year.
Annual leave is accrued at a rate of 2.5 days per month of completed service for staff.

Up to ten unused leave days may be carried over into the following fiscal year.

Full-time employees are entitled to 30 days of paid sick leave per calendar year.
Sick leave of more than three consecutive days requires a medical certificate from an accredited physician.
An additional 60 days of half-pay sick leave may be granted for serious or chronic conditions
Employees who require more than 90 days of consecutive sick leave may apply for an extended medical leave of absence of up to one year.
All employees undergo a formal performance review once per fiscal year
Employees rated 1 or 2 are placed on a Performance Improvement Plan with measurable goals and a review date 90 days later.
which is calibrated by the compensation committee in June and applied to July paychecks.
Female employees are entitled to ten weeks of fully paid maternity leave around the birth of a child.
Leave may begin up to four weeks before the expected due date.
to be taken within thirty days of the birth.
Adoption of a child under the age of three carries the same paid leave entitlements as biological parenthood
All open positions are posted on the university careers portal for at least fifteen business days before applications close.
References are conducted by phone and documented in writing.
Offer letters are valid for ten business days from the date of signing by the Provost or Chief Operating Officer.
Cash gifts of any amount are prohibited.
Acceptance of gifts from vendors with a value exceeding 500 SAR must be reported on the Annual Disclosure Form.
Base salaries are paid monthly on the 27th of each month.
Overtime is paid at 1.5 times the hourly rate for hours worked beyond 48 in a week.

Salary advances of up to two months of base pay may be requested in writing for documented hardship.
All new hires complete a mandatory orientation program within their first four weeks of employment.

Tuition reimbursement is available for degree programs aligned with the employee's role
A minimum grade of B is required for reimbursement.
Outside employment exceeding eight hours per week requires written disclosure to the supervisor and HR.

A first plagiarism offence results in a written warning and mandatory completion of the Academic Integrity Workshop within 30 days.
A second plagiarism offence within the same academic year results in failure of the course and a notation on the student's academic record that remains visible for two years.
carries an automatic course failure and academic probation for one semester.
including fabrication of experimental results or selective reporting of outcomes
Appeals against academic integrity sanctions must be submitted in writing to the Office of the Registrar within 14 calendar days of the decision being communicated.
Faculty members are required to report suspected violations of academic integrity within five business days of discovery using the official incident form available on the registrar portal.
Withdrawal after this deadline results in a grade of F.
are assigned only when a student has completed at least 70 percent of coursework but cannot finish due to documented medical or family emergency.
Incomplete coursework must be submitted within six weeks of the start of the following semester.

A minimum cumulative GPA of 2.0 is required to remain in good academic standing.
Students placed on academic probation must meet with their academic advisor within the first two weeks of the probationary semester and may not enroll in more than 12 credit hours.

Patrons with unpaid fines exceeding 100 SAR have their borrowing privileges suspended until cleared.
Each reservation has a maximum duration of three hours per day per student.
Withdrawal during the second week qualifies for a 75 percent refund of tuition.
Withdrawal during the second week qualifies for a 75 percent refund of tuition.
All refunds are credited to the original payment method within fifteen business days of the withdrawal being processed by the registrar.

IRB applications must be submitted at least six weeks before the planned start date of data collection.
Research data collected from human participants must be stored on encrypted university servers for a minimum of five years after publication.

The Merit Excellence Scholarship covers 100 percent of tuition for undergraduate students with a cumulative GPA of 3.75 or higher and is renewable annually.

Master's thesis defenses must be scheduled at least three weeks in advance and require a committee of three faculty members including the supervisor and one external examiner.
Doctoral dissertation defenses must be scheduled at least six weeks in advance and require a committee of five faculty members
The defense begins with a public presentation of approximately 30 minutes for master's students and 45 minutes for doctoral candidates

When a mailbox reaches 90 percent of its quota a warning email is sent.
At 100 percent the mailbox stops accepting new incoming messages.
after which they are permanently removed.
The 'UnivWiFi-Guest' network is open but rate-limited to 5 Mbps per device and requires daily reauthentication through the captive portal.
Passwords must be at least 12 characters long and contain a mix of uppercase letters
Passwords expire every 180 days.
Account lockout occurs after five consecutive failed authentication attempts within 15 minutes.
Lockout is automatically lifted after 30 minutes or can be cleared by IT support after identity verification.

Color printing counts as three pages against the quota for each color page printed.
Print jobs are held in the print queue for two hours before being deleted unreleased.
